# Cassette provenance — committee_memberships

- **Upstream URL**: `https://production.oknesset.org/pipelines/data/members/mk_individual/mk_individual_committees.csv`
- **Captured (UTC)**: 2026-04-23T21:10:08Z

- **SHA-256**: `dc249ce00bfc1d9a55e96532829e12f3cb1aa8b06939359ac9e06dce2908b8ea`

- **Recorder flags**: `--max-lines 101`

## Why this source (not Tier-1 OData)

`KNS_PersonToPosition` declares `CommitteeID`/`CommitteeName` columns but
has 0 populated rows out of 11,090 (the Knesset populates committee
memberships through a different pipeline). The oknesset open-government
mirror at `production.oknesset.org/pipelines/data/members/mk_individual/`
ships a dedicated `mk_individual_committees.csv` with 12,535 rows (April
2026) that IS populated.

## Join semantics

The CSV keys committee memberships by `mk_individual_id`, **not** by
`PersonID`. Resolving to the canonical `knesset_person:<PersonID>` UUID
requires the `mk_individual.csv` lookup (see
`tests/fixtures/phase2/lookups/mk_individual/sample.csv`).

`position_id` distinguishes chair (39/65), member (42), substitute
member (67), etc. The adapter records the position name as a
relationship property; it does not filter by role (all recorded
memberships yield a `MEMBER_OF_COMMITTEE` edge).

Regenerated by `make record-cassettes`. See
`.cursor/rules/real-data-tests.mdc` for the policy.
